title: [Feature]: Evaluate an IBM Db2 sink
problem: OCI GoldenGate lists IBM Db2 for i and Db2 for z/OS, while GoldenGate connectivity material also lists Db2 LUW. nats-sinks does not yet define a Db2 sink strategy.
proposal: Research an IBM Db2 sink covering Db2 LUW, Db2 for i, and Db2 for z/OS differences. The evaluation should identify driver support, SQL dialect, transaction behavior, upsert or merge support, identifier rules, mainframe connectivity constraints, and whether one connector can safely cover all Db2 variants.
users: Enterprise and mainframe integration teams that need controlled JetStream-to-Db2 persistence.
delivery_semantics: Success may be returned only after a Db2 transaction commits. Duplicate handling must use unique constraints and a documented merge/upsert strategy for the selected Db2 family.
security: Database credentials, host identifiers, schema names, and payloads must be redacted. Bind variables and least-privilege database grants are mandatory.
acceptance: Research determines whether one Db2 sink can support all variants.
Driver and licensing implications are documented.
Idempotency and SQL dialect constraints are specified.
Unit tests use fakes and no live Db2 services.
Documentation warns about platform-specific operational requirements.
tests: Add mocked driver tests and SQL generation tests for accepted dialects. Gate live Db2 tests.
documentation: Add Db2 sink evaluation documentation and roadmap updates.
closeout: Close after the research or implementation release is published.
